meta.name: OLBI Burnout Inventory
meta.description: The OLBI is a 16-item measure assessing two core dimensions of burnout: exhaustion and disengagement from work.
meta.author: Demerouti E, et al. (2003)
meta.reference: Demerouti E, et al. J Vocat Behav. 2003;62(3):473-488.
meta.instruction: Please indicate how much you agree with each statement about your work.

questions.text: I always find new and interesting aspects in my work.
There are days when I feel tired before I arrive at work.
It happens more and more often that I talk about my work in a negative way.
After work, I tend to need more time than in the past in order to relax and feel better.
I can tolerate the pressure of my work very well.
Lately, I tend to think less at work and do my job almost mechanically.
I find my work to be a positive challenge.
During my work, I often feel emotionally drained.
Over time, one can become disconnected from this type of work.
After working, I have enough energy for my leisure activities.
Sometimes I feel sickened by my work tasks.
After my work, I usually feel worn out and weary.
This is the only type of work that I can imagine myself doing.
Usually, I can manage the amount of my work well.
I feel more and more engaged in my work.
When I work, I usually feel energized.

ranges.label: Low Burnout
ranges.description: Low levels of burnout. This indicates that your current stress levels are manageable and you are coping well with daily challenges.
suggestions: Maintain healthy work habits
Continue current coping strategies
Continue using your effective stress management strategies
Regularly check in with yourself and engage in relaxation activities
Maintain a healthy work-life balance

ranges.label: Moderate Burnout
ranges.description: Moderate burnout levels. Mild stress is a normal part of life. Learning stress management techniques can help you handle it more effectively.
suggestions: Monitor your energy levels
Consider workload adjustments
Identify your main sources of stress and try to address them gradually
Learn time management and prioritization skills
Set aside 15-30 minutes daily for relaxation activities

ranges.label: High Burnout
ranges.description: High levels of burnout. Your stress level is elevated and may be significantly affecting your physical and mental health. Seeking support and making adjustments is recommended.
suggestions: Seek professional support
Evaluate your work situation seriously
Seek professional counseling support
Evaluate whether adjustments to your current work or life situation are needed
Learn emergency stress-relief techniques for high-pressure moments

exhaustion.label: Low
exhaustion.description: Low exhaustion. This indicates that your current stress levels are manageable and you are coping well with daily challenges.
suggestions: Continue using your effective stress management strategies
Regularly check in with yourself and engage in relaxation activities
Maintain a healthy work-life balance
Cultivate hobbies and interests that bring you joy
Nurture healthy social relationships

exhaustion.label: Moderate
exhaustion.description: Moderate exhaustion. Mild stress is a normal part of life. Learning stress management techniques can help you handle it more effectively.
suggestions: Identify your main sources of stress and try to address them gradually
Learn time management and prioritization skills
Set aside 15-30 minutes daily for relaxation activities
Maintain moderate exercise and healthy eating habits
Practice saying no and setting reasonable boundaries

exhaustion.label: High
exhaustion.description: High exhaustion. Your stress level is elevated and may be significantly affecting your physical and mental health. Seeking support and making adjustments is recommended.
suggestions: Seek professional counseling support
Evaluate whether adjustments to your current work or life situation are needed
Learn emergency stress-relief techniques for high-pressure moments
Ensure basic needs for sleep, nutrition, and rest are met
Consider temporarily reducing workload or seeking help to share responsibilities

disengagement.label: Low
disengagement.description: Low disengagement. This indicates that your current stress levels are manageable and you are coping well with daily challenges.
suggestions: Continue using your effective stress management strategies
Regularly check in with yourself and engage in relaxation activities
Maintain a healthy work-life balance
Cultivate hobbies and interests that bring you joy
Nurture healthy social relationships
